MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Jan-31-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nicholas Gioiello, Esq. 650-573-9500 of Chauvel & Glatt for Defendant.
No Other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BLUE BUS TOURS, LLC's MOTION FPR INSTANT ACTION INITS ENTIRETY.

Defendant Blue Bus Tours, LLC's motion to stay this action is granted in part. In September 2018, a putative wage-and-hour class action was filed against Blue Bus (CGC-18-569909). A year later, plaintiffs Shuntel Dickerson and Willie Payton - members of the putative class - filed their own suit regarding the same issues (CGC-19-579631). A tentative settlement of the class action has now been reached. Dickerson and Payton should soon learn its terms and decide whether they wish to opt out. Efficiency will be served by staying Case 579631 until after they opt out. If they do, Dickerson and Payton may then move to lift the stay. Dickerson and Payton rely on Baines v. Moores (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 445 for "a risk of witnesses forgetting facts, or dying." That seems unlikely here, given the relatively short time frame.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
